references.text: Ruhfel BR, PF Stevens & CC Davis. 2013. Combined morphological and molecular phylogeny of the clusioid clade (Malpighiales) and the placement of the ancient rosid macrofossil Paleoclusia. International Journal of Plant Sciences 174: 910-936

Ruhfel BR, V Bittrich, CP Bove, MH Gustafsson, CT Philbrick, R Rutishauser, Z Xi & CC Davis CC. 2011. Phylogeny of the clusioid clade (Malpighiales): evidence from the plastid and mitochondrial genomes. American Journal of Botany 98: 306-325
Stevens, PF. 1980. A revision of the Old World species of Calophyllum (Guttiferae). Journal of the Arnold Arboretum 61: 117-699

Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1193. = Calophyllum megistanthum Quisumb. & Merr. , PJS 37 (1928) 170. = Calophyllum ferrugineum Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 324 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 79. = Calophyllum glabrum Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 92 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 79. = Calophyllum mindanaense Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2684 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 80. = Calophyllum racemosum Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 198 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 80.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines, Taiwan. species.notes: In well-drained primary forests, thickets, often abundant. Fl. Feb-Jun, rarely Jan, Aug, Sep, Nov, Dec; fr. throughout the year. 0-1900m.

species.notes: BS 83930 Ramos & Convocar, NY, erroneously labeled as iso. Lowland streamside thickets on ‘iron deposit’ (may well be lateritic soil on ultramafics which is very common in the two areas). (?-)700m.

species.citation: (Blanco) Merr. , Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 266 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 80; Stevens, J. Arnold Arb. 61 (1980) 494; ≡ Ochrocarpos pentapetalus (Blanco) Fern.-Vill. , Fl. Filip., 3rd Ed. 4(Nov. App.) (1880) 17; ≡ Tovomita pentapetala Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 432; Type: Merrill Sp. Blancoanae 184, leg. Peña (A, neo; BO, F, GH, K, L, MO, NSW, NY, P, US, isoneo), Luzon: Lepanto prov. (a former geographic entity covering W Mountain Province and E Ilocos Sur). = Calophyllum amplexicaule Choisy ex Planch. & Triana , Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 4th Sér. 15 (1862) 281 ; S.Vidal, Phan. Cuming. Philip. (1885) 96; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 54; Merrill, PJS 5c (1910) 199; Type: Cuming 1212 (G, holo; BM, FI, K, MEL, iso), Luzon: Albay (according to Stevens, properly Ilocos Norte). var. pentapetalum. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Albay, Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Mountain Province, Pangasinan, Zambales, PALAWAN. Notes: Lowland dry forests, sometimes with pines, also in rocky sites in the littoral forest. Fl. Dec, Feb, Mar; fr: Nov, Dec, Mar. 0-620m. DAO category: Vulnerable. Native. var. cumingii (Planch. & Triana) P.F.Stevens, J. Arnold Arbor. 61 (1980) 497; ≡ Calophyllum cumingii Planch. & Triana, Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 4th Sér. 15 (1861) 259; S.Vidal, Phan. Cuming. Philip. (1885) 96; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 54; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 78; Type: Cuming 1077 (G, holo; BM, E, FI, G, K, L, MEL, NY, P, W, iso), Luzon: Albay (or Zambales?). = Calophyllum pseudotacamahaca Planch. & Triana, Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 4th Sér. 15 (1862) 270; S.Vidal, Phan. Cuming. Philip. (1885) 96; Rev. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 54; Type: Cuming 1047 (G, holo; BM, E, FI, G, K, L, MEL, MO, NY, P, W, iso), Luzon: Albay (or Zambales?) prov. = Calophyllum buxifolium Vesque, Epharmosis 2 (1889) 9; in DC., Monog. Phan. 8 (1893) 538; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 78; Type: Llanos s.n. (G, holo), Philippines, 1853. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CULION, GOLO, GUIMARAS, LUBANG, LUZON: Albay, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Pangasinan, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, PANAY. Notes: Habitats similar to var. pentapetalum. Fl. Nov-Mar, May; fr. Dec-Mar, May, Aug. 0-450(-917)m. Native. var. pulgarense (Elmer) P.F.Stevens, J. Arnold Arbor. 61 (1980) 498; ≡ Calophyllum pulgarense Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1786, as 'pulgarensis'; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 80; Type: Elmer 13217 (A, neo; BISH, BM, BO, E, F, FI, G, GH, K, L, LY, MO, NSW, NY, P, U, US, W, isoneo), Palawan: Palawan, Puerto Princesa, Mt Pulgar (Thumb Peak), 4250ft (1295m), May-1911. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN. Notes: Mossy summit scrub. Fl. May; fr. Dec. 1100-1300m. DAO category: Vulnerable. Native.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CULION, GOLO, GUIMARAS, LUBANG, LUZON: Albay, Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Mountain Province, NCR, Pangasinan, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, PALAWAN, PANAY.

species.notes: Known from Malbato (?Marche 381, A, P). Note by L.L.Co: This only known specimen from the Philippines is sterile. Calophylum dasypodum Miq. is a nom. nov. for Calophyllum lanceolatum Teijsm. & Binn.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 9c (1914) 454 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 81; Stevens, J. Arnold Arb. 61 (1980) 680; Type: Vanoverbergh 1466 (PNH, lost), Luzon: Mountain Province, Bauco, c. 1700m, 12-Sep-1918. Note by L.L.Co: From Merrill’s original description, Stevens distinguishes this taxon from Calophyllum soulattri in the following vegetative features: oblong, very coriaceous lamina that dries concolorous & nitid. An additional specimen from Catanduanes, BS 75484, may belong here.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. ?CATANDUANES, LUZON.
